records.ety: [LL. inculpatus, p. p. of inculpare to blame; pref. in- in + culpa fault. See Culpable.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inculpated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Inculpating (?).]

defs: To blame; to impute guilt to; to accuse; to involve or implicate in guilt. Contrasted with exculpate.
quotes: That risk could only exculpate her and not inculpate them -- the probabilities protected them so perfectly.
